events.event_description: First U.S. chief of mission to Greece, Charles Keating Tuckerman (Minister Resident), presented credentials, establishing the U.S. Legation in Greece.

evidence.quote: Charles Keating Tuckerman, Minister Resident. Appointed: 1868-03-11. Credentials presented: 1868-06-16. Presented recall on 1871-11-04.

events.event_description: Normal diplomatic relations with Greece were interrupted, ending the U.S. Legation's active operations.

evidence.quote: Edward Capps, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1920-06-18. Credentials presented: 1920-10-11. Normal relations interrupted on 1920-12-06.

events.event_description: Jefferson Caffery, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, presented credentials, resuming U.S. diplomatic representation in Greece at the legation level.

evidence.quote: Jefferson Caffery, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1922-02. Left post: 1923-05.

events.event_description: The U.S. Legation in Athens was closed as Germany occupied Greece, ending resident diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: Leslie E. Reed was serving as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim when the Legation in Athens was closed, July 14, 1941.

events.event_description: Anthony J. Drexel Biddle Jr. presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary near the Government of Greece in exile in England, re-establishing the U.S. Legation.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1941-11-13. Credentials presented: 1941-11-28. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1942-10-30. Note: Served near the Government of Greece established in England.

events.event_description: Biddle was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials, elevating the U.S. mission to Greece from a Legation to an Embassy.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1942-09-29. Credentials presented: 1942-10-30. Government of Greece transferred to Egypt on 1943-03-16.
